stories.title: HMRC Manual Changes: 17 July 2026
stories.summary: HMRC has published a new set of manual changes effective 17 July 2026, updating guidance across various tax areas.

stories.title: HMRC Major Projects Business Case Publications
stories.summary: HMRC has published business case documentation for its major projects, providing transparency on significant digital and operational transformation programmes.

stories.title: HMRC Consults on Simplifying Withholding Tax for Foreign Interest Payments
stories.summary: HMRC opened a consultation on proposals to simplify the process for withholding taxes on interest payments under double taxation agreements (DTAs), seeking industry feedback.

stories.title: British Girl, 15, Stranded in Rome for Six Weeks Due to New Passport Rules
stories.summary: A dual-national British girl missed six weeks of school after being stranded in Rome due to a recent Home Office change in passport policy. She is among several children affected by the new rules.

stories.title: Home Office Launches Undercover Policing Inquiry Consultation
stories.summary: The Home Office has opened a public consultation on the future of the Undercover Policing Inquiry, running from 16 July to 20 August 2026.

stories.title: Political Peerages July 2026 Announced
stories.summary: The Home Office has published the latest round of UK Life Peerages for July 2026, detailing newly appointed members of the House of Lords.

stories.title: UK Aid Spending Set to Fall by Around 42% by 2027–28
stories.summary: An independent watchdog report found UK aid spending is expected to fall by roughly £6.5 billion — a 42% reduction — from a 2023 record high to a historic low by 2027–28, as the UK cuts its aid budget from 0.5% to 0.3% of GNI.

stories.title: FCDO Publishes Annual Report and Accounts 2025–2026
stories.summary: The Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office has released its annual report and accounts for financial year 2025–2026, covering diplomatic activities and development spending.

stories.title: UK Commits to 90% Gender Equality Mainstreaming in New Development Approach
stories.summary: FCDO's updated modern development approach commits to mainstreaming gender equality across at least 90% of its work, raising ambitions for international development programmes.

stories.title: Customers Can Now Opt Out of Future Winter Fuel Payments
stories.summary: DWP has confirmed that customers in England, Wales and Northern Ireland can now contact the department to opt out of receiving future Winter Fuel Payments, following recent eligibility changes.

stories.title: PIP Statistics: Background Quality and Methodology Report Updated
stories.summary: DWP has updated its background quality and methodology report for Personal Independence Payment (PIP) statistics, providing detailed explanations of the data collection and assessment methodology.

agencies.name: DEFRA
agencies.description: Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs

stories.title: Government Publishes Future of Rural England Report
stories.summary: DEFRA has released a major report outlining challenges facing rural communities and the government's objective to ensure flourishing rural areas in England through targeted policy intervention.

stories.title: Government Commits to Deliver for Rural Communities
stories.summary: DEFRA announced a renewed commitment to deliver for rural communities, including new guidance on how government policy should account for rural needs and service provision.

stories.title: Government Responds to OEP Report on Environmental Improvement Plan
stories.summary: DEFRA published its response to the Office for Environmental Protection's 2026 report on progress improving England's natural environment between 2024 and 2025.

stories.title: MoD Launches Major Cancer Study for Armed Forces Community
stories.summary: The Ministry of Defence has launched a landmark study into cancer incidence among 1.4 million serving personnel and veterans, as part of efforts to improve long-term health outcomes for the armed forces community.

stories.title: MoD Employer Recognition Scheme Gold Awards 2026
stories.summary: Several local councils and organisations have received MoD's prestigious Employer Recognition Scheme Gold Awards 2026 for outstanding support of Armed Forces personnel and veterans.

stories.title: Dame Jayne-Anne Gadhia Appointed Chair of the Financial Reporting Council
stories.summary: The Department for Business and Trade announced the appointment of Dame Jayne-Anne Gadhia as the new Chair of the Financial Reporting Council, effective from 16 July 2026.

stories.title: Companies House Annual Report and Accounts 2025–2026 Published
stories.summary: Companies House released its annual report showing £12.1 million drawn down from DBT during the year to fund working capital, compared to £21.2 million the previous year, reflecting improved financial management.

stories.title: North of England Launches First-Ever Joint Trade Mission to India
stories.summary: Northern Mayors, joined by businesses and universities, launched the first-ever joint North of England trade mission to India, as total UK-India trade reached £47.9bn in 2025 — up 10% on the previous year.

agencies.name: DSIT
agencies.description: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

stories.title: DSIT Publishes Public Engagement Survey 2025/2026
stories.summary: DSIT released official statistics on how UK adults are engaging with science and technology in their day-to-day lives, as part of the annual Public Engagement Survey.

stories.title: DSIT Non-Consolidated Performance Related Pay 2024–2025 Published
stories.summary: DSIT released its annual transparency report on non-consolidated performance-related pay for senior staff for the 2024–2025 financial year.

stories.title: Advisers Recommend Offering All UK Teenagers Free Meningitis B Vaccine
stories.summary: A government advisory committee has recommended that all teenagers across the UK be offered a free meningitis B (MenB) vaccine at around age 15, following a series of fatal outbreaks — a significant expansion of the current NHS immunisation programme.

stories.title: All Newborns in England to Be Screened for Spinal Muscular Atrophy
stories.summary: Almost three-quarters of newborns in England are set to be tested for spinal muscular atrophy (SMA) from October under a pilot programme, with a view to extending universal newborn screening across the NHS.

stories.title: UK Plasma Approved for Use in Further Life-Saving Medicines
stories.summary: NHS Blood and Transplant announced approval for UK-sourced plasma to be used in additional life-saving medicines, following a risk assessment decision announced on 9 July 2026.

stories.title: Royal Mail Takes Action as New Digital Post Box Vandalised
stories.summary: Royal Mail's newly installed digital post boxes have become targets of vandalism, with boxes found covered in graffiti and tags. Royal Mail has responded with action to protect the new infrastructure.

stories.title: Royal Mail Changes 2nd Class Delivery to Alternate Weekdays
stories.summary: Royal Mail has begun delivering Second Class letters on alternate weekdays only (Mon/Wed/Fri or Tue/Thu), with no Saturday deliveries — a significant change to the universal service obligation.
